Farn Porrrrcel PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-56ffi . Fax (916) 322-0886
April22,2004
Lindsay Crane
Firm Administrator
The Gualco Group, Inc.
770 L Street, Suite 1440
Sacramento, CA 9581 4-3325
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-04-032a
Dear Ms. Crane:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of The Gualco Group, Inc. for
advice regarding the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Aci1.r This
letter in its entirety corrects and zupersedes the prior letter to you (Crane A-U-032)
which was issued on March 18,2004.
auEsTroN
Is The Gualco Group, Inc., a lobbying firm, correct in registering the Lake County
Sanitation Dishict as their new client and disclosing payments for lobblng as coming
from the Lake County Sanitation District even though the contract is with, and payments
for lobbying are received from, Criterion Planners Engineers?
CONCLUSION
Yes
I Govemment Code sectiors 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
iB_4


File No. A-04-032
Page No. 2
FACTS2
The Gualco Group, Inc., a lobblng firm, has recentlybeen employed to lobby on
behalf of Lake County Sanitation District ("District'). Specifically, The Gualco Group,
Inc. has been retained to attempt to secure additional gen*al obligation bond funds for a
project in the District entitled the Full Circleproject ("project'). The contract is between
The Gualco Group, Inc. and Criterion PlannerslEngineers ("Criterion'), project managers
employed by the Diskict to oversee the project. Payments to The Gualco Group, Inc. will
be made by Criterion. Although the contract will be with Criterion, The Gualco Group,
Inc. will be lobbying entirely on behalf of the interests of the District. If additional
general obligation bond funds are forthcoming they will bepaid to the District.
ANALYSIS
The Act regulates the activities of lobbyists, lobbfng firms and lobbyist
employers. Specifically, Chapter 6 of the Act requires lobbyists, lobbyist employers and
lobbying firms to register and file periodic reports disclosing money spent to influence
legislative or administrative action. (Sections 86100-86118.) The Gualco Group, Inc. is
a lobbying firm, meaning a business entity which '?eceives or becomes entitled to receive
any compensation...for the purpose of influencing legislative or adminishative action on
behalf of any other person, and any partner, olilnetr, officer, or employee of the business
entity is a lobbyist." (Section 82038.5.) As a lobbying firm, The Gualco Group, Inc. is
required to disclose both information about each lobblst employer with whom it
contracts to provide services and all payments received in connection with lobbying
activity. (sections 86104,86106, 86107, and 86114.) Thus, forpuposes of your
question, we must determine whether either Criterion or the District is a *lobbflst
ernployer" as defined in section 82039.5.
Section 82039.5 defines a lobblst employer, in relevant pd, is anyperson, other
than a lobblng firm, who:
"(a) Employs one or more lobblsts for economic consideration,
other than reimbursement for reasonable havel expenses, for the
purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action.,'
Using this definition, the Diskict is a lobbyist employer. 'We reach this
conclusion because the District is providing compensation, albeit through Criterion, to
The Gualco Group, Inc. for the "purpose of influencing legislative or administrative
action" in the form of securing additional general obligation bond funds. Criterion,s
hiring of The Gualco Group, Inc. as a lobbyist appears to be part of the professional
consulting services they provide when working on a project. Thus, Criterion does not
meet the definition of a lobblst employer in the context of hiring The Gualco Group,
Inc. in thatit is not employing or contracting with anyone to lobby on its behalf. (Section
82039.5.) When Criterion contracts with The Gualco Group, Inc. to provide lobbying
services for the District, Criterion is an intermediary for the delivery of compensation
2 Additional facts were provided in both telephone calls and e-mails.


I
l
File No. A-04-032
Page No. 3
from the District to The Gualco Group, Inc. (See Elsesser Advice Letter, No. I-98-255.)
When registering thenew client, The Gualco Group, krc. will disclose the Diskict and
when reporting payments, the payments will be disclosed as coming from the Diskict.
In addition, regulation 18613(a) states that a lobbying firm must disclose the
following:
"(l) The full namg address and telephone number of theperson or entity
making thepayment;
@ If the payment was made on behalf of anotherperson, the full name,
address and telephone number of the person on whose behalf the payment
wasmade ...."
Therefore, when payments me received from Criterion Planners/Engineers on
behalf of the Lake County Sanitation District, both entities must be disclosed on the
firm's quarterly reports.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
citt otz
By: Adrianne Korchmaros
Political Reform Consultant

Yes
